agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on continuing Granville Street pedestrian zone and patios
agenda_briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will vote on July 2, 2026 to extend the Granville Street Pedestrian Zone pilot through Labour Day weekend (September 6, 2026) and to allow FIFA-related temporary patios and public realm uses citywide to continue until September 6, 2026, where operationally feasible. The motion also authorizes up to $4 million in expenditures, including a $500,000 grant to the Downtown Vancouver BIA for programming. Approval requires at least two-thirds of all council members to vote yes under Section 206(1) of the Vancouver Charter.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on July 2, 2026 — if approved, businesses with temporary patio or street-use permits may continue operating through September 6, 2026.

key_dates.label: Special council vote on Granville Street pedestrian zone and patio extension
